What are passkeys?
Passkeys are a security measure that lets you log in the same way you unlock your device: with a fingerprint, a face scan, or a screen lock PIN. 
Why passkeys?
Passkeys are an added layer of security that protects your account against unauthorised access and phishing attacks.
How to create a passkey?
Go to Account Settings on [platform name] to set up your passkey. Each device can only save one passkey. However, iOS users may still see the Create passkey button due to iOS’s ability to save passkeys on other devices.
Where are passkeys saved?
Passkeys are saved in Google Password Manager for Android devices and in iCloud Keychain on iOS devices to help you sign in on other devices. 
What happens if I change my Deriv account email?
Even if you change your email address, you can still continue to log in to your Deriv account with the same passkey. 
Before using passkeys:
• Enable screen lock on your device.
• Sign in to your Google or iCloud account. 
• Enable Bluetooth.

Title: Deriv P2P balance
Body: Your Deriv P2P balance is made up of:
• Funds you received from buying USD on Deriv P2P.
• Profits from your trades, which you can sell to other Deriv P2P users.
• Deposits you made through non-reversible payment methods.Note: Funds deposited using reversible payment methods, like credit cards, Maestro, Diners Club, ZingPay, Skrill, Neteller, Ozow, and UPI QR, will not appear in your P2P balance.
Description: The current message we display when Deriv P2P users check their available Deriv P2P balance is not clear. To do: Improve the message. Make it clear that their Deriv P2P balance cannot be withdrawn or refunded back to the original payment method.
